\noindent Finally, we present results from the \ttblue{pps} evaluation method. For this purpose, we use audit values stored in the variable \ttblue{av2}. The total error amount reflected in \ttblue{av2} is 450,000.

\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\begingroup
\fvset{listparameters={%
  \setlength{\topsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\partopsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\parsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\itemsep}{0pt}%
}}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
ar <- evaluate(ar,
  av = myResults$av2,
  evalMeth = "pps"
)
ar$evalResults$`Error estimate`
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]
[1] 115448.3
\end{Verbatim}
\endgroup
\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\noindent A two-sided prediction interval around the PPS estimate is calculated according to Equations 5.6 and 5.7.
